title: Harden AI provider boundaries and diagnostics
goal: Poword should expose explicit provider and model configuration, use environment-driven secrets where possible, and leave enough diagnostics for operators to understand which AI path executed.
instructions: Review the current Poword AI integration and keep changes grounded in repository truth.
Prefer small, reviewable product changes over broad architectural rewrites.
Do not introduce harness runtime files, status files, or dashboard artifacts into Poword.
acceptanceChecks: Poword can determine provider and model without hardcoded values buried in AI call sites.
Operators can inspect basic diagnostics for AI requests without reading harness internals.
No harness runtime files or status directories are added to Poword.
expectedArtifacts: Updated Poword product code
Reviewable config or docs changes if needed

title: Extract deterministic review word selection from stored study activity
goal: Poword should choose review words from its existing dictionary and study-history data instead of relying on a hardcoded word list.
instructions: Add a small selection service that derives a review list from persisted study signals such as highlighted words, favorites, query history, and review results.
Keep the selection deterministic for the same database state by using explicit ranking and stable tie-break rules.
Update the article-generation flow so it can consume the selector output instead of embedding a sample vocabulary list in product code.
Cover the ranking and empty-state behavior with focused tests.
acceptanceChecks: The product can produce a review-word list from stored study data without editing source code.
The same underlying data produces the same ordered review-word output.
The article-generation flow no longer depends on a hardcoded demo vocabulary list.
Focused tests cover ranking or tie-break behavior and the no-candidate case.
expectedArtifacts: A review-word selection service
DAO support needed to read selection inputs
Focused tests for selector behavior

title: Add a manual article-generation entry point with topic and review-count inputs
goal: Poword should expose one supported way to run article generation from the command line using configured RSS topics and the selected review words.
instructions: Introduce a small application entry point that accepts a topic or RSS source choice and a requested review-word count.
Route the new entry point through the existing article-generation flow and the new review-word selector instead of requiring code edits in `main` methods.
Use the existing topic configuration keys where possible and print a concise run summary or a clear usage message for invalid inputs.
Document the supported invocation in repository-facing docs.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can start one article-generation run without changing source code.
The entry point accepts a configured topic choice and review-word count.
Invalid arguments return a clear usage or validation message instead of falling back to hidden defaults.
README or equivalent product docs show the supported invocation.
expectedArtifacts: A command-line generation entry point
Argument parsing or validation coverage
Updated usage documentation

title: Add a configurable daily generation launcher with duplicate-run protection
goal: Poword should be able to run one scheduled daily generation pass from configuration without producing repeated batches for the same day.
instructions: Add a small scheduler or scheduled-launch path that reads its topic, run time, and review-word count from configuration.
Prevent duplicate work by checking whether the day already has generated article output before starting a new run.
Fail fast with a clear operator message when required configuration or runtime prerequisites are missing.
Document the schedule settings and the duplicate-run behavior.
acceptanceChecks: The product exposes a configuration-driven daily generation mode.
A second launch on the same day does not generate a duplicate batch when the day's output already exists.
Missing schedule or runtime prerequisites produce a clear operator-visible failure message.
Documentation explains how the daily launcher decides whether to run or skip.
expectedArtifacts: A daily generation launcher
Duplicate-run guard logic
Configuration and usage documentation

title: Separate RSS source storage from finalized study article persistence
goal: Poword should preserve fetched source stories and finalized generated study articles as distinct records so one news item can move through the pipeline without title collisions or ambiguous reads.
instructions: Introduce an explicit persistence shape for source articles versus finalized generated articles, either through article fields or dedicated DAO methods, so the pipeline no longer overloads one row shape for both stages.

Update RSS ingestion to save source content through the source-side path and update article generation to save finalized Chinese and English output through the finalized side while preserving inserted-word data.
Make duplicate handling explicit for repeated RSS fetches of the same source article and repeated generation attempts for the same source article.
If the article table contract is not represented in the repo today, add the missing schema or a migration note alongside the existing SQL resources.
Cover the DAO and service lifecycle with focused tests for initial ingest, finalized save, and duplicate-source behavior.
acceptanceChecks: A fetched source article and its finalized generated study article can both exist for the same news item without ambiguous query results.
The generation flow reads source content deterministically and saves finalized output without relying on silent unique-constraint failures.
Repeated ingestion of the same source article does not create duplicate source records.
The repository includes enough schema or migration detail for maintainers to understand the article storage contract.
Focused tests cover source ingest, finalized save, and duplicate-source scenarios.
expectedArtifacts: Updated article persistence code
Article schema or migration documentation
Focused tests for the article lifecycle

title: Restore the generation quality gate and save actual inserted-word results
goal: Poword should only keep finalized study articles after the language and word-insertion checks run through the intended prompts and retry paths, and the saved metadata should reflect what was actually inserted.
instructions: Wire the language and word evaluators to the correct prompt contracts and response fields instead of reusing translation prompts.
Fix the retry flow so language failures route back to translation, word-insertion failures route back to insertion, and successful articles continue forward without duplicate work.
Save the words that were actually inserted into the finalized article record rather than only the originally requested review list.
Keep failure advice visible enough for maintainers to understand why a candidate article was retried or rejected.
Add focused tests with stubbed worker and evaluator outputs for pass, retranslate, and reinsert cases.
acceptanceChecks: Language and word-insertion evaluation use prompt keys that match the expected response fields for those steps.
A failed language check reruns the translation step, and a failed word-insertion check reruns the insertion step.
Successful articles complete the chain once and do not trigger extra retries.
Finalized article persistence records the words actually inserted into the saved article.
Focused tests cover pass, retranslate, and reinsert control-flow branches.
expectedArtifacts: Corrected evaluator and retry-flow code
Updated prompt contracts where needed
Focused pipeline tests

title: Add a generation preflight that blocks unusable runs before AI work starts
goal: Poword should validate the required runtime inputs for article generation up front so a maintainer gets one clear failure report instead of partial work and late exceptions.
instructions: Add a small preflight service for article generation that checks database connectivity, required AI configuration, prompt availability, RSS source reachability, and review-word availability before the pipeline starts.
Return a concise structured result with pass or fail status and human-readable failure reasons instead of letting downstream helpers fail independently.
Run the preflight at the start of the generation flow and stop before any article write when the environment is not ready.
Treat an empty review-word selection as a first-class preflight outcome with a clear message rather than silently continuing.
Cover the preflight outcomes with focused tests and document the supported checks.
acceptanceChecks: The generation flow performs one preflight before AI-backed work begins.
Missing API configuration, unreachable RSS input, unavailable prompts, database access problems, and empty review-word selection each produce a clear operator-visible failure reason.
When preflight fails, Poword does not begin article generation or write partial article output.
Focused tests cover successful preflight and representative failure modes.
Documentation explains what the preflight checks and how to fix each failure class.
expectedArtifacts: A generation preflight service
Preflight test coverage
Updated operator documentation

title: Keep batch article generation moving and record the latest outcome per source story
goal: Poword should continue processing the rest of a selected news batch when one source story fails, and it should persist the latest success or failure outcome for each source story.
instructions: Wrap per-source generation work so one failing story does not abort the rest of the selected batch.
Persist one latest outcome record tied to the source article, including outcome state, attempt time, and a concise failure summary or retry advice when available.
When a later retry succeeds for the same source article, clear or replace the stale failure outcome and keep the finalized article linkage deterministic.
Add focused tests for mixed success and failure in one batch and for a successful rerun after an earlier failure.
acceptanceChecks: If one selected source story fails during generation, later selected stories still run in the same batch.
The product can read the latest generation outcome for a source story without relying on console output or external log files.
A successful rerun for a previously failed source story replaces the stale failed outcome and preserves the linked finalized article behavior.
Focused tests cover mixed batch outcomes and outcome-state replacement on rerun.
expectedArtifacts: Latest-outcome persistence for source stories
Batch-resilience changes in article generation
Focused tests for success and failure transitions

title: Add a recent generation history and article detail lookup path
goal: Poword should expose one supported way to inspect recent source stories, their latest generation outcome, and any linked finalized study article without opening the database directly.
instructions: Add deterministic history queries that return recent source stories with their latest outcome and finalized-article presence.
Expose a small command-line read path for recent history and single-story detail lookup by source id or title fragment.
Show concise no-result and failed-outcome messaging so maintainers can tell whether a story never ran, failed, or completed.
Cover history ordering and detail lookup behavior with focused tests and document the supported usage.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can list recent generation history without inspecting the SQLite file directly.
History output distinguishes source stories with successful, failed, and not-yet-successful outcomes.
Detail lookup can show the source content and any linked finalized study article for one source story.
Focused tests cover deterministic history ordering and detail lookup behavior.

title: Add targeted retry for failed or unfinished source stories
goal: Poword should let a maintainer retry only failed or unfinished source stories instead of reprocessing stories that already completed successfully.
instructions: Add a retry path that selects eligible source stories from persisted data by source id, title fragment, or latest outcome state.
Reuse the existing generation flow and review-word selection rules, but skip already successful stories by default unless a force flag is explicitly provided.
Update the latest outcome and finalized article state when a retry runs, and print a concise retry summary with retried, skipped, and recovered counts.
Add focused tests for eligibility filtering, skip behavior, and successful recovery after a failed earlier attempt.
acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can retry only failed or unfinished source stories without re-running stories that already completed successfully.
A successful retry updates the story's latest outcome and keeps or creates the linked finalized article deterministically.
If no source stories are eligible for retry, the command returns a clear message instead of silently doing nothing.
Focused tests cover retry eligibility, default skipping of successful stories, and recovery after failure.
expectedArtifacts: A targeted retry command path
Eligibility and recovery logic for persisted source stories
Focused retry tests and usage documentation

title: Persist finalized article vocabulary links as first-class records
goal: Poword should keep the actual inserted words for each finalized study article as ordered linked records so later reading and study features do not depend on parsing one text field.
instructions: Add persistence for one row per finalized article and inserted word, preserving the insertion order reported by generation.
Populate the linked-word records when a finalized article is saved and replace stale records deterministically when the same source story is regenerated.
Keep the existing summary text only if it is still needed for compatibility, but make the linked records the authoritative source for downstream reads.
Add focused tests for initial save, rerun replacement, and zero-inserted-word cases.
acceptanceChecks: Finalized article persistence creates one deterministic ordered linked-word record per inserted word.
Saving a newer finalized article for the same source story replaces stale linked-word records instead of accumulating duplicates.
Product code can query the linked vocabulary for a finalized article without splitting a comma-separated string.
Focused tests cover initial save, rerun replacement, and zero-word behavior.
expectedArtifacts: Updated article persistence and schema for linked inserted words
Query support for article-linked vocabulary
Focused linked-word lifecycle tests

title: Preserve original RSS provenance on source stories
goal: Poword should retain the original source link and feed identity for each persisted source story so generated study articles can be traced back to the news item they came from.
instructions: Extend source-story persistence to keep the original RSS link and one stable feed or topic label alongside the existing deduplication key.
Update RSS ingestion so repeated fetches of the same story refresh provenance fields deterministically without creating duplicate source rows.
Expose the saved provenance on source-article reads so later product flows can show or export it without recomputing from raw feed input.
Document the expanded storage contract and add focused tests for save, rerun update, and legacy-schema compatibility.
acceptanceChecks: A persisted source story can report its original RSS link and feed or topic label from product storage.
Reingesting the same source story updates provenance in place without creating a duplicate source row.

title: Assemble a learner-ready article packet from persisted study data
goal: Poword should be able to build one structured reading packet for a finalized study article that combines the article text, linked vocabulary, and saved source context in a form that can be consumed outside the database.
instructions: Add a small read service that assembles a finalized article packet from persisted source, finalized, and linked-word records.

instructions: Add one supported read path that resolves a source story or finalized article from persisted records and writes the assembled packet as JSON to stdout or a requested file.

goal: Poword should let a maintainer export all successful study materials for one selected day into a stable folder tree that can be reviewed or shared without opening the database.

instructions: Add popup-only formatting rules that flatten whitespace, trim oversized translation or definition text, and preserve a tiny native footprint.
Keep full dictionary values available to CLI and persistence paths; only the popup preview should apply compact rendering rules.
Handle blank or partially populated dictionary rows gracefully so the popup still shows a stable minimal layout.
Add focused tests for long multiline values, whitespace-heavy fields, and compact fallback rendering.
acceptanceChecks: Long or multiline dictionary fields render as compact popup preview text without expanding the overlay into a large panel.
Popup compaction does not change the full values returned by CLI JSON or saved notebook writes.
Blank translation or definition fields still produce a stable minimal popup layout.
Focused tests cover compact rendering for long, multiline, and sparse dictionary content.
expectedArtifacts: Popup-specific preview formatting rules
Focused rendering tests
Short documentation for compact preview behavior

title: Save a previewed Rust selection from the native popup without re-reading the dictionary
goal: Once the popup is already showing a Rust-owned translation preview, accepting it should highlight and store that word through the previewed lookup state instead of repeating text normalization and dictionary lookup work.
instructions: Extend the Rust overlay state so the visible popup can retain the current previewed lookup payload together with the selection fingerprint it came from.
Add a Rust save path that can persist a previewed dictionary hit by stable word id while preserving the existing annotation-safe highlight behavior.
Reject stale or missing preview state before any notebook write and surface a short recoverable popup message instead of terminating the overlay.
Keep preview resolution read-only: no querylog or userwordinfo writes should happen until the user explicitly accepts the popup.
Cover the happy path and stale-preview failure path with focused overlay and vocabulary tests.
acceptanceChecks: Accepting a popup that already has a previewed word saves highlight state without performing a second lookup by selected text.
Preview attempts remain read-only until the popup accept action runs.
If the preview no longer matches the current visible selection, Poword does not write notebook state and shows recoverable feedback.
Focused tests cover preview-then-save success and stale-preview rejection.
expectedArtifacts: Updated Rust overlay state for preview-backed accepts
A Rust save-by-word-id path for previewed selections
Focused tests and operator docs for preview-backed save behavior

title: Ignore stale translation previews when the Windows selection changes mid-lookup
goal: The popup should only ever show preview text for the user's current selection, even if the selection changes or clears while a preview lookup is still in flight.
instructions: Make translation preview resolution selection-scoped with a stable fingerprint or request token carried from popup request through preview completion.
Drop any completed preview result that no longer matches the current selection instead of repainting the popup with stale text.
Hide or keep the popup quiet when the selection clears before preview completion.
Apply the same stale-result protection to both passive UI Automation captures and the explicit clipboard fallback path.
Add focused tests for rapid selection changes and clear-before-preview-completes behavior.
acceptanceChecks: A preview result for an older selection never appears after the user has moved to a different selection.
Clearing the selection before preview completion does not leave stale preview text visible.
Clipboard fallback previews follow the same stale-result rules as passive selections.
Focused tests cover changed-selection and cleared-selection preview races.
expectedArtifacts: Selection-scoped preview lifecycle logic in the Rust overlay
Race-focused overlay tests
Updated docs describing stale-preview handling

title: Clamp the Poword popup to the active monitor instead of the virtual desktop
goal: For system-wide Windows use, the popup should stay visible and local to the monitor where the selection or cursor anchor lives rather than clamping against one virtual-screen rectangle.
instructions: Derive popup clamp bounds from the monitor nearest the selection rectangle instead of the full virtual screen bounds.
Preserve the current compact edge-clamping rules within that monitor so the popup stays visible near left, right, top, and bottom edges.
Use the same monitor-aware placement for clipboard fallback anchors based on the current cursor or focused element location.
Keep the implementation native and lightweight without introducing a browser surface or heavy UI shell.
Add focused tests for at least one multi-monitor layout and document the monitor-aware placement behavior.
acceptanceChecks: A selection on a secondary monitor produces a popup clamped within that monitor rather than against the whole virtual desktop.
Selections near monitor edges still keep the popup fully visible.
Clipboard fallback placement uses the correct monitor bounds for its anchor.
Focused tests cover monitor-aware placement in a multi-monitor scenario.
expectedArtifacts: Monitor-aware popup placement logic

title: Expose a Java compatibility command for the Rust translate-and-save path
goal: Poword should let maintainers invoke the existing Rust-owned vocabulary save flow from the Java entry point so notebook highlight writes can be validated through the same boundary the Windows overlay already uses.
instructions: Add one Java operator command that delegates to the Rust companion `translate` path and passes the selected text through unchanged.
Reuse the existing companion-resolution and error-reporting helper instead of reimplementing dictionary lookup or `userwordinfo` writes in Java.
Print the Rust JSON result without renaming fields, and keep invalid-selection or missing-word failures operator-visible with no partial Java fallback write.

acceptanceChecks: A maintainer can trigger one Rust-backed translate-and-save run from the Java entry point without starting the Windows overlay.

title: Suppress dead-end Poword actions when Rust preview already knows the selection will miss
goal: Poword should stay low-friction by refusing to surface a clickable overlay action for compact selections that the Rust preview path already rejects as invalid or missing from the dictionary.

title: Wait for passive selections to settle before showing the Poword action

instructions: Add a small stability gate for passive UI Automation selections so the overlay only previews and surfaces an action after the same normalized selection has remained stable for a short bounded interval.

title: Shift the Windows overlay to an adaptive poll cadence when idle
goal: Poword's always-on Rust overlay should stay lightweight on Windows by polling aggressively only while a popup or likely selection is active, and backing off when the desktop remains idle or unsupported.
instructions: Keep the always-on process fully Rust-native and Windows-only; do not add a tray shell, browser runtime, or Java-owned loop.

Introduce a small cadence model in the overlay runtime that distinguishes fast active polling from slower idle polling based on runtime state such as a visible popup, pending feedback, a recently eligible selection, or repeated idle/protected/unsupported polls.
Preserve the current selection gating, preview, save, and handled-selection suppression rules; this slice should change poll timing, not product decisions.

title: Serialize native-popup notebook mutations so one live selection has one in-flight write
goal: Poword should keep the always-on popup trustworthy by allowing only one notebook mutation sequence to own the current live selection at a time, so rapid cross-action clicks cannot enqueue conflicting writes or scramble popup state.
instructions: Reuse the overlay runtime's existing pending-action and sequence state to give each popup mutation one exclusive owner tied to the current live selection identity.

When a notebook mutation is already pending, suppress conflicting follow-up mutation clicks instead of queueing another SQLite write, and keep the current pending label or feedback stable until the owner action resolves or is invalidated.
Keep notebook work off the polling tick and UI thread; this slice is about action ownership and gating, not moving work back into synchronous paths.
Add focused overlay tests for rapid cross-action input on the same popup so the repo proves only one mutation request is emitted during one pending window.
acceptanceChecks: While one popup notebook mutation is pending, a conflicting second popup action on that same live selection does not enqueue a second write.

title: Drop superseded notebook mutation results after a newer popup action wins
goal: Poword should ignore out-of-order notebook mutation completions so an older worker result cannot overwrite newer popup state or move the active preview cache backward for the same still-visible selection.
instructions: Tag popup notebook-worker requests with a monotonic mutation token and only adopt a completion when its token still matches the popup's current mutation owner.

If a newer retry or later popup action has already taken ownership for that same live selection, discard the older completion locally instead of reopening stale feedback or refreshing preview caches from outdated post-write state.
Do not attempt to roll back already committed SQLite writes in this slice; only prevent stale completion adoption inside overlay state.

acceptanceChecks: An older notebook mutation completion cannot overwrite a newer popup state for the same unchanged selection once a later action has already won ownership.

title: Invalidate pending popup mutations when overlay guards dismiss the live selection
goal: Poword should clear in-flight popup mutation context when the overlay is dismissed by app, session, or suppression guards, so a late worker completion cannot resurrect stale UI after the desktop context has already moved on.
instructions: Route popup dismissal and suppression events through one pending-mutation invalidation path that clears the live mutation owner, pending-action presentation state, and any action-scoped UI refresh expectations for the dismissed selection.
Keep the already launched worker isolated; if it later returns after the popup was dismissed, treat that completion as stale and leave the overlay quiet until a fresh eligible selection appears.
Cover at least foreground-app change, full-screen suppression, and non-interactive session invalidation with focused overlay tests or runtime unit tests.

acceptanceChecks: If the popup is dismissed by an app switch, full-screen guard, or non-interactive session event, a later completion from the old mutation does not reopen popup feedback or refresh caches for that old selection.
